calendar.matters:
Mtn For Atty Fees
Off Calendar Nov-05-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS PI-CHING YEN and ERIC LIEN's Motion For Attorney's Fees is Granted. The requested rate of $400 per hour is reasonable. Mr. Walker has been the attorney representing Lien and Yen in this case for several years, including before he moved his office to Maryland. The fact that Mr. Walker requested $300 per hour five years ago in connection with attorney fees in another motion in another one of the cases between these parties does not preclude an award of $400 per hour in November 2013. The Court finds that the hours spent from May 10, 2013 through July 26, 2013 are reasonable hours spent for work on the successful anti-SLAPP motion that is the subject of this request for attorney fees. These hours total 72.6 hours. In addition, the Court finds that counsel should be awarded attorney fees for 5 hours spent in preparing this motion for fees. The Court does not award attorney fees for the first anti-SLAPP motion which was denied by the Court on May 28, 2013. The grounds for the first and second motions (and the oppositions thereto) were sufficiently different such that it would not be appropriate to award attorney fees for time spent on the unsuccessful motion. Attorney fees are awarded in favor of Defendants and against Plaintiffs in the amount of $31,040 to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of the entry of order. (CCP 425.16(c).) Defendants' request for judicial notice is denied. The Court does not take judicial notice of unpublished decisions of the Court of Appeal or the contents of an order in an unrelated case. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2010 Off Calendar

Motion To Compel Defendant/Cross-Complainant Eric Lien To Appear For Deposition And Produce Documents And Extend Discovery (Added To 4/20/10, 10:30am, Dept. 612 Calendar Purs.To Order Of 4/8/10
Off Calendar Apr-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Motion To Compel Defendant/Cross-Complainant Eric Lien To Appear For Deposition And Produce Documents And Extend Discovery - GRANT. The matter came on the calendar pursuant to an ex parte motion filed April 8, 2010, with papers filed in connection with that ex parte motion deemed the moving papers here. An opposition was filed March 15, 2010, which is late. However, in the interests of justice I have considered the opposition. I have balanced on the one hand the relatively minimal showing of diligence made by moving party, which ordinarily would counsel denial of the motion, with the obvious necessity of deposing a party in the case and the attendant prejudice to the plaintiffs should I not grant the motion. On balance, because the one day delay in notice causes no prejudice to plaintiffs, and because denial of motion would punish plaintiffs for the apparent negligence of their counsel, I have determined to grant the motion.

Lien is ordered to appear for his deposition and comply with the request to bring documents not subject to valid objection. The parties should meet and confer on a suitable date for the deposition and if the date is not determined at the hearing of this motion then the parties may jointly contact Judge Karnow by phone through his clerk to resolve any differences on the date. Prevailing party to prepare order. JUDGE CURTIS E.A. KARNOW, NOT REPORTED
